stem: A programmer breaks code into procedures and functions (subroutines).
model_answer: Both are named blocks of code that can be called from elsewhere in the program. A function returns a value to the code that called it and is usually used within an expression (e.g. x = square(3)). A procedure performs a task but does not return a value. Using subroutines reduces duplication, makes code easier to read and lets the same logic be tested and reused.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Compare a function and a procedure, and give one benefit of using subroutines.

mark_points.text: a function returns a value to the caller
a procedure performs a task/actions but does not return a value
benefit of subroutines: reduces duplication / easier to read / reusable / easier to test
